ety: v. adj., of the measure faʻīl, fr. نَقِيَ 'to be pure,' &c.
def: adj. Pure, clean; excellent, exquisite; of the finest quality.

s.m. A chief, a leader; — an intelligent person; — a servant or herald whose business it is to proclaim the titles of his master, and to introduce those who pay their respects to him; — an adjutant, or aide-de-camp; — a procurer, an agent.
more: n. m. 1. A herald. 2. A herald who proclaims the titles of his master, and introduces persons who come to pay their respects to him. 3. An adjutant or aide-de-camp . 4. A procurer; an agent.

s. m. 1. A chief, a leader. 2. An intelligent person. 3. A servant whose business it is to proclaim the titles of his master, and to introduce those who pay their respects to him. 4. An adjutant or aide de camp.

ety: act. part. of نقد 'to separate good from bad coin,' &c.
def: s.m. One whose business it is to examine coin and ascertain its goodness; an assayer.
more: (part. act. of نقد Heb. ) s. m. A person whose business it is to examine coin and ascertain its goodness, an assayer.

ety: v. adj. fr. نقه; see naqāhat
def: adj. Weak, feeble, debilitated; faint; languid.

ety: act. part. of نقل 'to transport; to relate,' &c.
def: s.m. A reporter, relater, reciter, narrator; — a transcriber, copier; — a delineator, painter.
more: (part. act. of نقل) s. m. A reporter, reciter, narrator, relater, delineator.

def: s.m. A trough made of a hollowed palm-tree (in which they make a strong wine from dates, &c.); — a canal; trough; — a hollow dug in the ground, or in a stone; — a small groove in a date-stone (from which the stalk grows); — adj. Very small; of little moment; poor and despised, the meanest
more: 1. s. m. A trough of a hollowed palm tree in which they make a strong wine of dates, &c. 2. The spot on the back of a date stone through which the young plant shoots; (hence) adj. Very small, of little moment, meanest. نقير وقطمير naḳīr-o-ḳit̤mīr, All the minutiæ, every particle, great and small.
subs: s.m. All the minutiæ, every particle, great and small; — adj. & adv. Minute; particular, exact; — minutely, exactly; to the point.

ety: v. adj. fr. نکر 'to be penetrating, or sagacious, &c.; to be ignorant'
def: adj. Sagacious, penetrating, ingenious, shrewd; — ignorant.
more: n. m. P. nok point. A jecting corner.
(from نکر Heb. and Aram. ) adj. 1. Sagacious, penetrating, ingenious. 2. Ignorant.

def: s.m. Name of one of the two angels (the other being Munkar) believed by the Muslims to examine the dead in the tomb.
more: s. m. Name of an angel. See منکر.

ety: for A. نکرة nakirat, fem. of nakir, q.v.
def: adj. & s.m. Undetermined, unrestricted, indefinite, or generic (noun); — an indefinite noun, a common noun.
more: n. m. ( Gram .) A common noun.

ety: act. part. of نقص 'to be defective,' &c.
def: adj. Defective, imperfect, deficient, wanting (in); unfinished, incomplete; mutilated; unsound, bad, vicious, worthless; — inexpert
more: adj. نقص diminishing, injuring. 1. Defective; wanting; mutilated. 2. Unsound; bad; vicious; worthless. nāqis-ul-aql , adj. Of unsound mind. nāqis k. , v. a. To vitiate; render defective; mutilate. nāqis h. , v. n. To deteriorate; to be vitiated.

(part. act. of نقص) adj. Defective, imperfect, deficient, mutilated, inexpert. ناقص عقل nāḳiṣ-aﻌḳl, adj. Deficient in understanding, foolish, ignorant. ناقص کرنا nāḳiṣ karnā, To mutilate. ناقص هونا nāḳiṣ honā, To want, to fail.
subs: or nāqiṣ-ʻaql, adj. Deficient in understanding or judgment; of unsound mind; foolish, ignorant, silly, stupid
v.t. To render defective; to vitiate; to mutilate
v.n. To be or become defective, or deficient, &c.; to be vitiated; to deteriorate; to want, to fail.

def: v.t. corr. of nakhiyānā, q.v. v.t. To scratch, to claw.

ety: v. adj. fr. نقض; see naqẓ
def: adj. Adverse, contrary, opposite; inimical; — conflicting, discordant; inconsistent (with); — s.m. An enemy; — s.f. Opposition; contrariety; — animosity, enmity.
more: adj. See zid . 1. Adverse; contrary; opposite (خلاف). 2. Conflicting; discordant; inconsistent. naqīz , n. f. 1. Opposition; contrariety. 2. Animosity; enmity.
(from نقض analog. to Chald. ) 1. adj. Adverse, contrary, opposite. 2. s. m. An enemy.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Of or relating to the nose; nasal; — one who speaks through the nose; — a nasal twang; — (in gaming) a cowrie thrown upon its face (lit. 'nose'); — a single cowrie; — the ace (at dice, or cards)
more: n. m. 1. One kauṛī , q. v. 2. The ace. nakkā-dūā, nakkā-mūṭh , n. m. Gambling ( juā ). nakkī par lagānā yā rakhnā , v. a. To stake; hazard.
s. m. The ace at cards, or on dice. نکا دوا nakkā dūā, A kind of game at dice.
subs: (dūʼā = S. dyūta+ka); or nakkā-mūṭh (˚mūṭh = S. mushṭi), s.m. A game of hazard with cowries, &c., odd or even (cf. S. mushṭi-dyūta)
(-ko), To give a nasal twang (to); to snuffle
or nakkī-par lagānā, v.t. To hazard; to stake.

ety: intens. n. fr. نقد 'to separate good from bad coin,' &c.
def: s.m. A person whose business it is to examine money and ascertain its goodness, a cambist, an assayer; — an adept.
more: (adj. superl. of نقد) s. m. A person whose business it is to examine money and ascertain its goodness; an adept, assayer.

ety: intens. n. fr. نقل 'to copy, imitate,' &c.
def: s.m. A mimic, mime; an actor, a player (syn. bhāṅṛ).
more: (adj. superl. of نقل) s. m. A mimick, an actor, a player.

def: s.f. Acting; mimicry.
s.f. An actress; a mimic.
s.m. A kettle-drum
more: n. m. نقر A kettledrum. 1. Tan kī tanak sarāë meṅ tanak na pāyo chain, Sāṅs naqārā kūṅch kā bājat hai din rain . No rest within this narrow inn my frame me greets, All night and day this breath the marching order beats. 2. Naqqāre bāj, damāme bāj gaë . Prov. Saying. naqqārā ulaṭnā v. n. ( Met .) To be turned (the scales or tables). naqqārah bajāte phirnā , v. n. To proclaim; blazon abroad. naqqārā bajā-ke yā naqqāre kī choṭ , adv. Publicly; openly. naqqārah h. , v. n. 1. To be like a drum. 2. To be pot bellied. Peṭ naqqārah ho rahā hai .
s. m. A kettledrum. نقاره بجاتي پهرنا naḳāra bajāte phirnā (dakh. i. q. مشـﮩور کرنا) To publish, make celebrated (?).
subs: v.n. 'The drum to turn over'; (met.) the scales or the tables to be turned
To go round proclaiming by beat of drum; to proclaim, to blazon abroad
or naqqāre-kī ćoṭ, adv. By beat of drum, publicly, openly.
wety: Learned borrowing from Arabic نَقَّارَة (naqqāra). Doublet of نَگَاڑَا (nagāṛā).

def: s.m. corr. of نقاره naqqāra, q.v. s.m. A kettle-drum
subs: v.n. 'The drum to turn over'; (met.) the scales or the tables to be turned
To go round proclaiming by beat of drum; to proclaim, to blazon abroad
or naqqāre-kī ćoṭ, adv. By beat of drum, publicly, openly.

def: s.m. One who beats the kettle-drum (naqqāra, q.v.), a drummer.
more: n. m. One who beats the kettledrum.
s. m. One who beats the kettledrum.

def: s.m. = naqqārćī, q.v.
more: s. m. One who beats the kettledrum.

def: s.m. The place at the porch of a palace where the drums are beaten at stated intervals (see naubat-ḵẖāna).
more: n. m. The porch of a palace where the drums are beaten at stated intervals.
The place at the porch of a palace where the drums are beaten at stated intervals.

def: s.m. A painter; drawer; limner; draughtsman; designer; embroiderer; gilder (of books); carver; engraver; sculptor, statuary.
more: n. m. A painter; draftsman; designer; limner.
(adj. superl. of نقش) s. m. A painter, drawer, draftsman, sculptor, statuary, carver, embroiderer, &c.

def: s.f. Painting; drawing; designing; embroidery: carving; engraving; sculpture, statuary.
more: s. f. Drawing, painting, sculpture, statuary, embroidery.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نَقَّاشِی (naqqāšī). By surface analysis, نَقَّاش (naqqāś, “painter”) + ـی (-ī).

def: s.f. 'The having the nose cut off'; — disgrace, infamy.
s.f. Speaking through the nose; nasal sound or twang (see also s.v. nakkā, of which nakkī is the fem.): — nakkī-mūṭh = nakkā-mūṭh.
more: n. f. One who speaks through the nose. naksel h . To be puzzled, harrassed. nak-kaṭī , n. f. Disgrace; ill-fame. nak-ghisnī , n. f. Rubbing one's nose on the ground in the act of prostration. nak-ghisnī karnā , v. a. To beg; entreat.
s. f. Speaking through the nose, nasal sound.

def: s. f. Name of a game.

ety: Prk. नक्कउओ, from S. न्यक् (rt. नि+अञ्च्)+कृ
def: adj. Degraded, disgraced; infamous, low, vile, contemptible, base
more: adj. 1. Disgraced. 2. Infamous. nakkū , n. m. 1. One who has a large nose. 2. One on whom every body turns up his nose. nakkū banānā , v. a. To make one an object of scorn. nakkū bannā , v. n. To become infamous. nakkū k. , v. a. To point the finger at; give a bad name.
adj. Infamous. ¶ nako, adv. (dakh.) Not, do not, must not.
subs: or nakkū karnā, v.t. To render infamous, or contemptible; to make (one) an object of scorn
v.n. To make oneself infamous, to become infamous, or contemptible.

def: adj. & s.m. Having a large nose, big-nosed; — one who has a large nose
subs: adj. & s.m. Big-nosed, bottle-nosed; — a big-nosed man.

def: s.f. Transporting, carrying from one place to another, removing; transportation; removal; translation; — transmission; — transfer, alienation (of property); — transcribing; copying; imitating; mimicking; acting; mimicry; — imitation of a copy; a transcript, copy; a duplicate; — a history, narrative, relation, report, account; tale, story; anecdote; fable
more: n. f. 1. Change of place, removal. 2. Transfer, alienation (of property). 3. A copy; transcript. 1. Naql rā che ٴaql? Prov. Copying is not a mark of intelligence. 2. Naql-i-kufr kufr na-bāshad . P. Prov. To take an infidel's name is not to be an infidel. 4. Mimicry; acting. 5. A narrative. 6. A story; tale; an anecdote. naql-ul-naql . G. G. Copy of a copy. naql ba jinsihī, naql mulābiq-i-asl . G. G. An exact counterpart of the original; a true copy. naql-i-parvāna , ( Fac .) A brother-in-law (سالا). naql khilāf , G. G. 1. A false copy. 2. A misquotation. naql k. , v. a. 1. To make a copy. 2. To represent; personate; act. 3. To relate; narrate. naql musaddaq , An attested or authenticated copy. naql-i-makān , n. m. 1. The first short stage of a journey. This first stage is often made long before the actual journey in order to take advantage of the auspicious hour which may notrecur for a …

s. f. 1. A history, narrative, tale, story, anecdote, fable. 2. Copying, mimicking, acting. 3. Transcribing. 4. Imitation of a copy, transcript, copy, counterpart. 5. Transporting from one place to another, removing, translation. نقل کرنا naḳl karnā, To relate, narrate, to copy, to act, play, perform, or represent (a character). نقل مکان naḳl-i-makān, 1. The first stage of a journey, or halting place at a very little distance, where they remain some time to collect what may have been forgotten of the requisites for travelling. This first stage is often made, long before they really mean to set off, for the sake of seizing a lucky moment to commence a journey in. 2. Transmigration. نقل نويسي naḳl-navīs-ī, s. f. Copying. ¶ nuḳl, s. What is eaten with wine, as fruits or sweetmeats, a dessert: (dakh.) A sort of sweetmeat.
subs: or naql mut̤ābiq-ě-aṣl, An exact counterpart of the original, a true copy
v.t. To relate, narrate; — to make a copy (of, -kī), to copy; to imitate, to mimic; to act, to represent or personate (a character)

s.m. Moving from a place, removing, removal; — emigration; — conveying (to another place); — transmigration; — dying; — the first stage of a journey, or halting-place at a very little distance (where they remain some time to collect what may have been forgotten of the requisites for travelling; this first stage is often made long before they really mean to set off, seizing a lucky moment to commence a journey in); — naql-ě-makān karnā, v.n. To remove; to emigrate, &c.
v.n. To be related, to be said; — to be copied (from, -se)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نَقْل (naql), borrowed from Arabic نَقْل (naql).

subs: or naql mut̤ābiq-ě-aṣl, An exact counterpart of the original, a true copy

To remove; to emigrate, &c.
v.n. To be related, to be said; — to be copied (from, -se)

def: adj. Transmitted; handed down; traditional; — imitated; fabricated, artificial; fictitious, spurious, counterfeit, false; — s.m. A mime, mimic, an actor, a jester, buffoon; — a narrator, relater, story-teller.
more: adj. 1. Fabricated; counterfeit; false. 2. Traditional; handed down.
s. m. A narrator, relater, storyteller. adj. Traditional, copied, artificial, fictitious.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نقلی (naqlī). Equivalent to نقل (naql, “imitation”) + ی (ī).

def: s.m. A mime, mimic; an actor, a jester, a buffoon.
(pl. of an unused A. form naqlīyat, fem. of naqlī), s.f. pl. Narratives, stories, tales, fables.
s.f. Spurning, rejecting, despising; chiding; reproach, blame; scorn, contempt; rejection.

def: s.m. Defect; deficiency; diminution, decrease, wane; detriment, damage; blemish, flaw, unsoundness, weakness (as in a title, &c.); injury, harm, mischief (i.q. nuqṣān); — naqṣ-ě-badr, s.m. Wane (or variation) of the moon
more: n. m. See اوگن (3—6.) 1. Defect. 2. Decrease; diminution. 3. Infirmity (in a title). nuqs-i-badr , ( Ast .) Variation of the moon. nuqs-i-jismānī , G. G. A material defect. nuqs-i-aql , Uusound mind; unsoundness of mind. nuqs-i-faislah , Defect of judgment. nuqs-i-qāte , n. m. A vital defect. nuqs-i-qānūnī , n. m. A legal defect. nuqs-i-qarār , n. m. Breach of contract. nuqs kamī-i-qīmat , n. m. Error of defective valuation. nūqs nikālnā , v. a. To pick out faults.
s. m. Defect, diminution, detriment, blemish, injury, harm, mischief.
subs: s.m. Defective service; — breach of duty
s.m. A vital defect; a decided flaw
(-meṅ), To pick out defects, or faults, &c. (in).

def: s.m. Painting; colouring; drawing; designing, &c.; — delineation; — embroidery; — a painting, a picture; portrait; drawing; a print; a carving, an engraving; a map, or plan (com naqsha); a design; — an impression; a stamp; a mark; — a magic square; — a charm; — a style of singing, or a kind of song (invented by the people of Khurāsāṇ)
more: n. m. 1. An impression; a stamp; mark. 2. A painting; drawing. 3. An engraving; a carving. 4. A magic square; a charm. naqsh biṭhānā , See sikkā biṭhānā. naqsh-bandiyā , n. m. A Mah. sect of religious mendicants. naqsh-i-pā , n. m. A foot-print; track. naqsh-i-dīvār , adj. Still as a statue; thunder-struck; dumb-foundered. naqsh k. , v. a. Paintings; pictures. 2. Decoration; embellishment. naqsh ho jānā , v. n. To be stamped or engraven (on the mind).

s. m. Painting, embroidering, print, impression, mark, engraving, carving, picture, drawing, delineation, map, portrait. نقش بند naḳsh-band, part. Creating, imagining; creator, imaginer, embroiderer, painter. نقش پا naḳsh-i-pā, Footstep. نقش ديوار naḳsh-i-dīwār, Thunderstruck, confounded so as to appear like a picture on the wall. نقش کالحجر naḳsh ka-l-ḥajar, or more properly کالنقش في الحجر ka-n-naḳsh fi-l-ḥajar (lit. def: s.m. more: n. m. 1. A delineation; picture. 2. A plan; design. 3. A form; blank form; exemplar. 4. A figured statement; table; return. 5. A register; muster roll. 6 A map; chart. 7. Features; visage. Voh chāl chanchal, voh nazreṅ jādū, voh piyārī sūrat, voh khūb naqsha, Adāëṅ bāṅkī gazab tarah kī, voh tirchhī chitvan bhī kuchh tamāsha! Nazir. That wanton gait, those magic eyes, that lovely face and form, Those graceful airs so wonderful, those side-long glances warm! 8. Condition; predicament (حال 1). Kyā naqshe haiṅ? The bamboo which fowlers use to entangle game by applying birdlime to the top of it. نل چلانا nal-chalānā, Setting the bamboos in motion, denotes a magical practice adopted for the discovery of theft. Two pieces of split bamboo, of equal lengths, are applied to one another side by side, and held by two men, one at each end. It is said that any persons taken at random may be employed for this purpose. Then the magician pronounces certain incantations, the efficacy of which is pretended to be such, that the bamboos spontaneously move towards the place where the thief or the stolen goods are, and drag the men along with them. نل در نل nal dar nal, One tube within another. 3. Bhaī! do Jagdīs kahāṅ te āë, kaho kaun bhirmāyā? Allah, Rām, Karīmā, Keso, Har, Hazrat nām dharāyā? Eke kāl sakal sansārā, eke nām hai jagat jagpiyārā . Kabir. Whence, brother, come two Lords God, say? Who, say, hath led thee all astray? Allāh and Rām, Karīmā, Keso, Hari and Hazrat, names but below: One death, one name beloved avow! 2. ( nām-āvarī ) Good name; repute; renown; fame. nām uchhālnā, yā raushan k. , v. a. ( Ironic .) To bring disgrace upon. nām uchhālnā, yā nikalnā , v. n. To be destroyed (one's good name); to become a by-word. Nāhaq uchhal rahā hai chhināle meṅ merā nām, Mujh se ziyādah āp kī bhenā haiṅ, kam nahīṅ . For naught a loose one I am known, No less than me's your sister own. nām-bācha…

s. m. Name, appellation, character, fame, reputation, honour. نام آور nām-āwar, adj. Famous, renowned. نام آوري nām-āwar-ī, s. f. Fame, renown. نام خدا nām-i-ḵẖudā, In Godʼs name, bravo! good heavens! نامدار nām-dār, Famous, celebrated. نامداري nām-dār-ī, Celebrity. نام دهرنا nām dharnā, To name, to fix a name on (particularly, a bad name), to nickname, miscal: کبـﮩي بولين عقيق اورگـﮩ نگين لعل ٿهـﮩراوين يـﮩ نا شاعر تري هونٿهون کو کيا کيا نام دهرتي هين Kab-hī boleṅ aﻌḳīḳ aur gah nagīn-i-laﻌl ṭhahrāweṅ; yih nā-shāʼiﻌr tire honṭhoṅ ko kyā kyā nām dharte haiṅ. These poetasters sometimes call your lips cornelians, and sometimes signets of ruby; in how many ways do they miscal them! نام دهروانا nām dharwānā, 1. To be defamed. 2. To give a name to a child. نام دينا nām denā, To give name to, to make conspicuous. نام ڐبونا nām ḍubonā, to lose oneʼs honour or reputation. نام رکهنا nām rakhnā, To …

wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀡𑀸𑀫 (ṇāma), from Sanskrit नामन् (nā́man), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *Hnā́ma, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hnā́ma (compare Persian نام (nâm)), from Proto-Indo-European *h₁nómn̥ (“name”). Cognate with Punjabi ناں (nāṉ) and English name.

The ceremony of feeding a child for the first time when about six or seven months old. 2. ( namak-chashī ) The interchange of trays of sweetmeats between the families after betrothal. namak-harām , adj. 1. Ungrateful. 2. Disloyal. namak-harāmī , n. f. 1. Ingratitude. 2. s. m. (dakh. nimak ) 1. Salt. 2. ( met. ) Spirit, animation. 3. Bread, subsistence. نمک بند namak-band, adj. s.f. corr. of nāk, q.v. (i.q. S. नक्, base नज्), suffix (added to verbal roots, and subst., to form adj. of quality), Affected (with), inflamed (with), full (of), -ful; laden (with), &c. (e.g. ḵẖishm-nāk, 'inflamed with anger'; g̠am-nāk, 'affected with grief, sorrowful').
s.m. (f. -ī), Little child, little son (an expression of endearment).

def: s.m. See s.v. nān. adv. No, not, nay (i.q. nā, q.v.). adv. (In Urdū) The inseparable form of نه na, q.v
more: s. m. 1. An allowance of money or land to zamīndārs, ḳānūngos, &c. for subsistence. 2. Land granted to servants for their subsistence.

def: adj. Belonging to nānkār, q. v.
adj. = nanhā, q.v.
more: adj. 1. Small; tiny. 2. Little; young. nannā ; Bhoj nānhā , n. m. nannī , n. f. 1. A child; a son. 2. A mere child. 3. A darling. Aise hī tum nanne ho! nannā kātnā ; Bhoj. nānhi kaṭāval , v. n. 1. To spin fine. 2. To use sparingly; to be frugal. 3. To be miserly. nannī jāt , Low castes. nanne miyāṅ , n. m. A fabulous spirit worshipped by Mah. women. nannī , n. f. 1. A small or young girl or woman. 2. A ninny. ( Ironic ). nannī-kā , n. f. lit . born of a ninny. A simpleton; fool. Aisā hī tū nannī kā hai!

def: adj. (f. -ī), Small, diminutive, tiny, wee; — little, young; — neat, natty; — petty; low; — s.m. A mere child, a child, a wee thing; — a darling, a pet
more: adj. Small, diminutive, neat, natty, tiny. (Perhaps from s. न्यून ).
subs: (or nannā) kātnā, v.t. 'To spin fine'; (fig.) to use sparingly; — v.n. To be frugal; — to be parsimonious or miserly
(or nannī) jāt, s.f. A low caste
or nannī, s.f. A small, or young, girl or woman; — (ironic.) a ninny.

def: adj. (f. -īṅ) = nannhā, q.v.

def: s.f. Measure; measurement; survey
more: n. f. S. मापन, Measure; measurement; survey. Nāp na tol, bhar de jhol! Mendicants. Nor weights nor yard you need to get, you 've only got to fill my wallet. nāp biddyā ; A. masāhat , n. f. ( Geom .) Mensuration. nāp kā pūrā , Of full measure, height, etc. Ghoṛā yā javān nāp kā pūrā . nāp kī ikāī , ( Mech .) The unit of measurement. be-nāp, be-māp , adj. Unmeasured; not surveyed. jamī nāp, jamā paimāna , Standard or full measure. chaltā paimāna, chaltī nāp , Somewhat below the standard measure. kaṭtī nāp , (the cutting line measure) abbreviation, kan . A. sahm (an arrow) S. chhedan rekhā . (Geom.) The secant.
s. f. Measure. ناپ جوکهـ nāp-jokh, Measuring and weighing.
subs: s.f. The science of measuring, mensuration
